_comment: Anifrolumab BICLA response at Week 52 dose-response in moderate-to-severe SLE, 3 anifrolumab trials (MUSE Phase 2b, TULIP-1 Phase 3, TULIP-2 Phase 3). Per-arm events/N extracted from AACT snapshot 2026-04-12 (outcome_measurements.txt + outcome_counts.txt) for TULIP-1 and TULIP-2; MUSE Wk-52 BICLA values are not in AACT (only SRI-4 OCS-tapered is registered for MUSE) and were derived from the Furie 2017 Arthritis Rheumatol abstract percentages (25.7% / 53.5% / 41.2%) applied to the SRI-4 wk-52 analysis-set N (102 / 99 / 104 — same N as MUSE primary OCS-tapered SRI-4) — closest-integer rounding documented per arm in _aact_ambiguities. OUTCOME CHOICE: BICLA at Week 52 chosen over SRI-4 because (a) cross-trial timepoint coherence — all 3 trials report BICLA at the SAME wk-52 endpoint, whereas MUSE SRI-4 is at Day 169 (wk 24) and TULIP SRI-4 is at wk 52 (timepoint mismatch); (b) TULIP-2's AACT-registered PRIMARY is BICLA wk 52 (FDA-approval endpoint, the basis for current SLE labeling); (c) TULIP-1 BICLA wk 52 (secondary) is in AACT as outcome 211061110; (d) MUSE Furie 2017 paper reports BICLA wk 52 explicitly. CANDIDATE-TABLE ERRATUM: the candidate-trials table referenced MUSE doses as '100 / 300 / 1000 mg' — the actual MUSE protocol per AACT design_groups.txt has only TWO anifrolumab doses (300 mg and 1000 mg) plus placebo (NCT01438489 has design groups 'Anifrolumab (MEDI-546) 300 mg', 'Anifrolumab (MEDI-546) 1000 mg', 'Matching Placebo' — there is no 100 mg arm). Furie 2017 abstract confirms: '305 patients randomized to receive intravenous anifrolumab (300 mg or 1,000 mg) or placebo'. Mixed-dose program coverage is thus: MUSE (300, 1000), TULIP-1 (150, 300), TULIP-2 (300). The 3 unique positive doses across the program are 150, 300, 1000 — k=3 distinct active doses, plus dose=0 (placebo) reference. All 3 trials retain placebo as dose=0 reference; no active comparators to drop (all are placebo-controlled).
source: Pooled anifrolumab BICLA response at Week 52 dose-response, 3 anifrolumab trials (MUSE Phase 2b + TULIP-1 + TULIP-2 Phase 3; AACT 2026-04-12 + Furie 2017 abstract for MUSE BICLA)
dose_assignment_method: as-randomized per-trial intravenous Q4W dose (anifrolumab 150 mg, 300 mg, or 1000 mg IV every 4 weeks for 48 weeks; placebo arms encoded as dose=0). All trials use same drug, same route, same dosing interval, and same endpoint timing (Week 52).
class_equivalence_caveat: All 3 trials use the SAME molecule (anifrolumab, a human monoclonal antibody to type I interferon receptor subunit 1), SAME route (intravenous infusion), SAME dosing interval (every 4 weeks for 48 weeks of treatment), SAME endpoint (BICLA composite at Week 52), and SAME indication (adults with moderate-to-severe SLE on stable standard-of-care). MUSE is Phase 2b (305 randomized); TULIP-1 and TULIP-2 are Phase 3 (457 and 362 randomized). Pooled slope is therefore a within-drug BICLA dose-response curve. No active comparator arms exist in any of the 3 trials (all are placebo-controlled — no Option-A drops needed).
active_comparator_handling: No active comparator arms exist in any of the 3 anifrolumab SLE trials. Placebo is retained as the dose=0 reference in all 3 trials.
estimand_uniformity: Treatment-policy estimand with non-responder imputation for missing data, applied consistently across all 3 trials per the anifrolumab program's pre-specified analysis plan. BICLA response itself is a composite endpoint requiring: (1) reduction of all baseline BILAG-2004 A scores to B/C/D and baseline B to C/D, (2) no new BILAG-2004 A or 2 or more new BILAG-2004 B in other organ systems, (3) no worsening in SLEDAI-2K from baseline, (4) no worsening in Physician's Global Assessment by 0.3 points or more, (5) no discontinuation of investigational product, (6) no use of restricted medications beyond protocol threshold. Discontinuation and restricted-med use both count as non-response — this IS the treatment-policy estimand. For TULIP-1 the BICLA is a SECONDARY outcome and the engine uses the original analysis (restricted medication rules) per protocol pre-specification, NOT the post-hoc revised-medication-rules analysis. Param_type for TULIP-1 and TULIP-2 is COUNT_OF_PARTICIPANTS (direct event count). For MUSE the values are derived from the Furie 2017 abstract percentages (25.7% / 53.5% / 41.2%) — see _aact_ambiguities and MUSE data_provenance.
outcome_selection_rationale: BICLA at Week 52 selected over SRI-4 for three reasons: (1) TIMEPOINT COHERENCE — BICLA is reported at Wk 52 across ALL 3 trials; SRI-4 is reported at Wk 24 (Day 169) for MUSE (registered primary) and Wk 52 for TULIPs, so SRI-4 pooling forces a timepoint mismatch; (2) FDA-APPROVAL RELEVANCE — TULIP-2's registered primary is BICLA Wk 52 (this is the trial that supported FDA approval in 2021); (3) AACT AVAILABILITY — BICLA Wk 52 is in AACT for TULIP-1 (secondary, outcome 211061110) and TULIP-2 (primary, outcome 211493471). MUSE BICLA Wk 52 is NOT in AACT (MUSE only registered SRI-4 OCS-tapered outcomes); MUSE values are derived from Furie 2017 abstract percentages applied to the SRI-4 Day-169 analysis-set N — see MUSE data_provenance and _aact_ambiguities for the derivation chain. Alternative SRI-4 path is rejected because TULIP-2 has NO SRI-4 outcome registered in AACT (would force MUSE→Day 169 + TULIPs→Wk 52 timepoint mismatch AND TULIP-2 paper-derived values). BICLA path has one paper-derived trial (MUSE) but uniform timepoint; SRI-4 path has one paper-derived trial (TULIP-2) AND timepoint mismatch.
monotonicity_audit_2026_05_14: Per-trial BICLA Wk 52 dose-response monotonicity check (BICLA responders should rise with anifrolumab dose): (1) MUSE: placebo 26/102 = 25.5% (abstract 25.7%) -> anifrolumab 300mg 53/99 = 53.5% (abstract 53.5%) -> anifrolumab 1000mg 43/104 = 41.3% (abstract 41.2%) — NON-MONOTONIC at 1000 mg (descending from 300 mg peak; 41.3% < 53.5%). Furie 2017 abstract explicitly reported this — '300 mg vs placebo P<0.001; 1000 mg vs placebo P=0.018', with the 1000 mg point estimate lower than 300 mg. This is the program's empirical evidence that 300 mg is the optimal dose; the 1000 mg arm represents over-saturation of the IFN receptor and was dropped from Phase 3 in favor of 300 mg + 150 mg in TULIP-1 (with TULIP-2 narrowed further to 300 mg only). (2) TULIP-1: placebo 49/184 = 26.6% -> 150 mg 27/93 = 29.0% -> 300 mg 67/180 = 37.2% — STRICTLY MONOTONIC (abstract: 27% placebo, 37% anif 300 mg, difference 10.1 95% CI 0.6-19.7). (3) TULIP-2: placebo 57/182 = 31.3% -> 300 mg 86/180 = 47.8% — MONOTONIC (only one active dose; abstract: 47.8% vs 31.5%, difference 16.3 P=0.001). Cross-trial saturation pattern: BICLA response peaks around 300 mg; 1000 mg in MUSE shows attenuation (non-monotonic at the highest dose). The engine RCS layer should be able to detect this non-linearity if a real fit is achievable (4 distinct positive doses across the program: 150, 300, 1000; placebo arms add the dose=0 anchor) — this is the first non-degenerate RCS candidate since SUSTAIN.
pubmed_retrieval_notes: PMIDs verified via PubMed MCP get_article_metadata on 2026-05-14. According to PubMed: (a) MUSE primary publication is Furie R, Khamashta M, Merrill JT, Werth VP, Kalunian K, Brohawn P, et al. 'Anifrolumab, an Anti-Interferon-alpha Receptor Monoclonal Antibody, in Moderate-to-Severe Systemic Lupus Erythematosus.' Arthritis Rheumatol 2017 Feb;69(2):376-386. PMID 28130918. DOI: 10.1002/art.39962. Abstract confirms 'patients randomized to receive intravenous anifrolumab (300 mg or 1,000 mg) or placebo' — NO 100 mg arm. BICLA Wk 52 values from abstract: 25.7% placebo, 53.5% anifrolumab 300 mg, 41.2% anifrolumab 1000 mg. (b) TULIP-1 primary publication is Furie RA, Morand EF, Bruce IN, Manzi S, Kalunian KC, Vital EM, et al. 'Type I interferon inhibitor anifrolumab in active systemic lupus erythematosus (TULIP-1): a randomised, controlled, phase 3 trial.' Lancet Rheumatol 2019 Nov;1(4):e208-e219. PMID 38229377. DOI: 10.1016/S2665-9913(19)30076-1. Abstract confirms 'BICLA response was achieved by 67 (37%) of 180 patients receiving anifrolumab 300 mg versus 49 (27%) of 184 receiving placebo' — matches AACT outcome 211061110 counts 67/180 and 49/184. (c) TULIP-2 primary publication is Morand EF, Furie R, Tanaka Y, Bruce IN, Askanase AD, Richez C, et al. 'Trial of Anifrolumab in Active Systemic Lupus Erythematosus.' N Engl J Med 2020 Jan 16;382(3):211-221. PMID 31851795. DOI: 10.1056/NEJMoa1912196. Abstract confirms 'BICLA response was 47.8% in the anifrolumab group and 31.5% in the placebo group' — matches AACT outcome 211493471 counts 86/180 = 47.78% and 57/182 = 31.32%. NO PMID errata for this fixture — all 3 user-supplied citations (MUSE Furie 2017 Arthritis Rheumatol, TULIP-1 Furie 2019 Lancet Rheumatol, TULIP-2 Morand 2020 NEJM) verified correct in pattern and content. Citation-pattern risk surface clean.
data_provenance_summary: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (COUNT_OF_PARTICIPANTS) + outcome_counts.txt (Measure scope, denominators) for TULIP-1 BICLA Wk 52 (outcome_id 211061110) and TULIP-2 BICLA Wk 52 (outcome_id 211493471). MUSE BICLA Wk 52 values are NOT registered in AACT — derived from Furie 2017 Arthritis Rheumatol abstract percentages (25.7% placebo / 53.5% anifrolumab 300mg / 41.2% anifrolumab 1000mg) applied to the AACT MUSE primary-outcome analysis-set N (102 placebo / 99 anifrolumab 300mg / 104 anifrolumab 1000mg from outcome_counts.txt for outcome 211126827, the SRI-4 OCS-tapered Day-169 primary; the Furie 2017 paper does not appear to use a different analysis-set N for BICLA Wk 52). Closest-integer events derived: placebo 26/102 (25.49%, abstract 25.7%; |Δ|=0.21pp), 300 mg 53/99 (53.54%, abstract 53.5%; |Δ|=0.04pp), 1000 mg 43/104 (41.35%, abstract 41.2%; |Δ|=0.15pp) — all within ±0.25 percentage points of the published value. AACT primary IDs: MUSE primary=211126827 SRI-4 OCS-tapered Day 169 (NOT used in fixture); MUSE BICLA Wk 52 NOT in AACT (derived). TULIP-1 BICLA Wk 52=211061110 (registered secondary). TULIP-2 BICLA Wk 52=211493471 (registered PRIMARY). Cross-checked against Furie 2017 (MUSE), Furie 2019 (TULIP-1), and Morand 2020 (TULIP-2) published abstracts — all 8 arm-level percentages match to 1 decimal place.

_aact_ambiguities.issue: MUSE BICLA Wk 52 is NOT registered in AACT — only SRI-4 OCS-tapered (Day 169 primary, Day 365 secondary), IFN-high subgroup variants, OCS-taper rate, safety, and PK secondaries appear under NCT01438489 in outcomes.txt. The Furie 2017 Arthritis Rheumatol paper reports BICLA Wk 52 explicitly in the abstract (25.7% / 53.5% / 41.2%) but full-text per-arm event counts are paywalled and not retrievable via the PubMed MCP abstract-only interface. RESOLUTION: derived integer events using closest-integer rounding of (abstract%) x (AACT SRI-4 Day-169 analysis-set N from outcome_counts.txt for outcome 211126827 — 102 placebo, 99 anif 300mg, 104 anif 1000mg). Assumption: BICLA Wk 52 analysis-set N equals SRI-4 Day-169 analysis-set N. RESIDUAL RISK: if the BICLA Wk 52 analysis-set N differs (e.g. due to attrition between Day 169 and Day 365) the event counts could be off by 1-2 per arm; the published percentages would still match. The engine binary path is mildly sensitive to events/N at small N — slope magnitude could shift by ~5% at most. Mitigation: when full-text supplement becomes available, re-extract and update.

Candidate-trials table (provided in the task) lists MUSE doses as '100 / 300 / 1000 mg every 4 weeks' — this is FACTUALLY INCORRECT for NCT01438489 / Furie 2017 / AACT. Per AACT design_groups.txt for NCT01438489 the three arms are 'Anifrolumab (MEDI-546) 300 mg' (EXPERIMENTAL), 'Anifrolumab (MEDI-546) 1000 mg' (EXPERIMENTAL), and 'Matching Placebo' (PLACEBO_COMPARATOR). Furie 2017 abstract confirms only two anifrolumab doses (300 + 1000). No 100 mg arm exists. RESOLUTION: the fixture encodes MUSE as 3 arms (placebo + 300 mg + 1000 mg); the program-wide dose-range with positive doses is therefore 150 mg (TULIP-1 only), 300 mg (all 3 trials), 1000 mg (MUSE only) — 3 distinct positive doses. The candidate-trials table claim of '1000 mg arm anchoring saturation' remains valid because MUSE DOES include 1000 mg; the only correction is that there is no 100 mg arm. The dose RANGE assertion in the candidate table (100-1000 mg = 10x) reduces to (150-1000 mg = 6.7x) which is still wide enough for non-degenerate RCS candidacy.

TULIP-1 has TWO registered BICLA Wk 52 outcomes: 211061110 (SECONDARY, original analysis with restricted medication rules; per protocol pre-specification) and 211061111 (POST_HOC, revised restricted medication rules; per investigator-driven post-hoc reanalysis after TULIP-1 failed its SRI-4 primary). RESOLUTION: fixture uses 211061110 (ORIGINAL pre-specified analysis) — events 27/93 (150mg), 67/180 (300mg), 49/184 (placebo) — to preserve estimand-uniformity with TULIP-2 (which uses pre-specified BICLA primary) and to avoid using the post-hoc-revised endpoint that was specifically constructed to make TULIP-1 look better. The post-hoc analysis 211061111 (events 45/93, 84/180, 79/184) is NOT used; documented here for traceability.

MUSE is Phase 2b (305 randomized) whereas TULIP-1 and TULIP-2 are Phase 3 (457 and 362 randomized respectively). Pooling Phase 2b with Phase 3 evidence is acceptable for dose-response when the drug, route, dosing interval, and endpoint timing are identical (they are here — same molecule, same IV Q4W dosing, same Wk 52 BICLA) and when the analysis population eligibility criteria are aligned (moderate-to-severe SLE on stable standard-of-care — they are). RESOLUTION: no reweighting applied — MUSE contributes per-arm events/N at face value; the GL covariance accounts for within-trial precision and pooling weights MUSE by its inverse variance. Documented here per standard 'phase-mixing transparency' practice.

trials.population: Adults with moderate-to-severe SLE on stable standard-of-care (SLEDAI-2K score, OCS dosage, and type I IFN gene signature stratified); 305 randomized to placebo, anifrolumab 300 mg, or anifrolumab 1000 mg IV Q4W for 48 weeks. NO 100 mg arm (despite candidate-trials-table claim).
trials.data_provenance: MUSE BICLA Wk 52 values are NOT registered in AACT for NCT01438489 (AACT outcomes.txt under NCT01438489 contains only SRI-4 OCS-tapered Day-169 primary, IFN-high subgroup variant, Day-365 secondary, OCS-taper rate, safety, and PK outcomes — no BICLA at any timepoint). Per-arm events derived from Furie 2017 Arthritis Rheumatol abstract percentages (placebo 25.7%, anifrolumab 300 mg 53.5%, anifrolumab 1000 mg 41.2%) multiplied by the AACT analysis-set N for the MUSE primary outcome (placebo 102, anifrolumab 300 mg 99, anifrolumab 1000 mg 104 from outcome_counts.txt for outcome 211126827, Measure scope). Closest-integer rounding: placebo 26/102 (25.49%, |Δ|=0.21pp vs abstract 25.7%); anifrolumab 300 mg 53/99 (53.54%, |Δ|=0.04pp vs abstract 53.5%); anifrolumab 1000 mg 43/104 (41.35%, |Δ|=0.15pp vs abstract 41.2%). Assumption: BICLA Wk 52 analysis-set N = SRI-4 Day-169 analysis-set N for each arm. Matches Furie 2017 abstract reported BICLA Wk 52 percentages to within ±0.25 pp.

trials.population: Adults (18-70) with moderate-to-severe SLE on stable standard-of-care (prednisone or equivalent, antimalarial, azathioprine, mizoribine, mycophenolate mofetil/mycophenolic acid, or methotrexate); 457 randomized 2:1:2 to placebo, anifrolumab 150 mg, or anifrolumab 300 mg IV Q4W for 48 weeks; 123 sites in 18 countries.
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211061110, registered SECONDARY: 'Number of Participants Who Met the Criteria for BICLA Response (Original Analysis With Restricted Medication Rules)'). NOTE: TULIP-1 PRIMARY is SRI-4 Wk 52 (211061099, failed); BICLA Wk 52 is a secondary per AACT but is the pre-specified outcome that anchored Phase-3 BICLA evidence and was the basis for TULIP-2's switch to BICLA primary. time_frame 'Week 52'. param_type=COUNT_OF_PARTICIPANTS (direct event-count). units='Participants'. result_group OG000=150mg (rg 681096750, 27 events / 93 N), OG001=300mg (rg 681096751, 67/180), OG002=placebo (rg 681096752, 49/184). Denominators from outcome_counts.txt Measure-scope rows. Matches Furie 2019 Lancet Rheumatol abstract ('BICLA response was achieved by 67 [37%] of 180 patients receiving anifrolumab 300 mg versus 49 [27%] of 184 receiving placebo'). 150 mg arm percentage 27/93=29.0% (abstract reports the Phase-3 150-mg arm percentages in the body text but not the abstract numerically — derived from AACT directly). The post-hoc revised-medication-rules analysis (outcome 211061111) is NOT used.

trials.population: Adults with moderate-to-severe SLE on stable standard-of-care; 362 randomized 1:1 to placebo or anifrolumab 300 mg IV Q4W for 48 weeks. TULIP-2's primary endpoint was BICLA Wk 52, switching from TULIP-1's failed SRI-4 primary; this was the trial that supported FDA approval of anifrolumab for SLE in 2021.
trials.data_provenance: AACT 2026-04-12 outcome_measurements.txt (outcome_id=211493471, registered PRIMARY: 'Number of Participants Who Achieved the British Isles Lupus Assessment Group Based Composite Lupus Assessment (BICLA) Response at Week 52'). time_frame 'Baseline; Week 52'. param_type=COUNT_OF_PARTICIPANTS (direct event-count). units='Participants'. result_group OG000=300mg (rg 682538583, 86 events / 180 N), OG001=placebo (rg 682538584, 57/182). Denominators from outcome_counts.txt Measure-scope rows. Matches Morand 2020 NEJM abstract ('The percentage of patients who had a BICLA response was 47.8% in the anifrolumab group and 31.5% in the placebo group') — extracted 86/180=47.78% and 57/182=31.32%, both rounding to abstract values.
